aids.title: Leftist Property: Left is Longer
aids.content: LEFT is LONGER: the left subtree always has an s-value >= right subtree. This means the shortest path to null is always on the RIGHT side. Operations follow the right path, which is short = O(log n).
aids.forRemembering: The core leftist tree property and why operations are efficient

aids.title: Leftist Tree as a Left-Leaning Tower
aids.content: Imagine a tower that always leans left. All the heavy weight (longer paths, more nodes) is on the left side. The right side is kept intentionally short. When you need to do work (merge, insert, delete), you walk down the short right side, so you finish quickly.
aids.forRemembering: Why leftist trees keep the right path short and how this aids efficiency

aids.title: All Operations Reduce to Merge
aids.content: Insert = merge with a single-node tree. Delete-min = merge left and right subtrees of root. This means if you understand merge, you understand ALL leftist tree operations. Merge follows right paths, swaps children if violated, updates s-values.
aids.forRemembering: That merge is the fundamental operation in leftist trees
